threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus suddenly stops and everyone leaps off at the random unmarked stop. That’s just how Dalston works.
The other night, saw a bloke chatting up a stray cat at the bus stop—kept saying he understood its life struggles. Bizarrely touching.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard the sound of a street musician mixing traditional tunes with techno at Sonnenallee. Only here, right?

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain can you overhear a heated debate about the best vegan döner while standing next to a street artist painting a mural of a cat in a spacesuit.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they take the 14 Mission and complain about the bus delays like it’s a rite of passage, not a shock.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to navigate the Muni with a pet iguana. No one batted an eye. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think they can order a 'café con leche' any time, but locals know it's strictly breakfast—or you're just asking for a baffled look.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their bewildered expression while trying to decipher the metro map during rush hour. Always looking lost.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look confused when the bus driver says 'mind the gap' in a thick Cockney accent. The locals all chuckle at your expression.
Caught a guy in a full panda suit getting on the N133 last night. He just sat there, sipping his coffee, completely unfazed.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Sitting at the Kottbusser Tor, I watched a street artist juggle while a guy on a bike shouted for someone to catch his coffee. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar serving cocktails named after famous protests, like 'Blockupy Breeze.' It's quirky, even for Berlin.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they're locals when they dodge the BART Civic Center evacuation chaos like pros, while newcomers just stare in confusion.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a ukulele in the Tenderloin last night. He played 'Somewhere Over the Rainbow' while the bus was stuck in traffic.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are just for selfies. Locals use them for evening gatherings, exchanging stories over a tinitus or two.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them hesitating at the tram stop, checking their phones while trying to figure out which line goes where—classic rookie move.
